title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin violated NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman in Concord, NH, in May 2024.
Court ordered Lufkin to have no contact with the victim and imposed a $5,000 fine, $4,000 suspended for compliance.
Lufkin pled guilty to assault charges, receiving a 12-month sentence with part suspended for three years.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox's trade plans are described as 'fluid,' with potential moves before the August 3 deadline.
The team aims to continue its winning momentum, having secured 10 consecutive victories, impacting their trade strategy.
Boston's playoff odds increased to 44.3% after recent successes, influencing decisions on potential trades like Aroldis Chapman.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is listed for sale at $12.5 million as owners retire after 25 years.
The property includes a pizzeria and bar, takeout area, staff housing, and parking facilities at two locations.
Owners Evan and Maria Marley seek a buyer to continue the business, with plans for Evan to assist in transitioning.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's soccer team left Boston after over a month, concluding their stay at Four Seasons Hotel since June.
The team departed for Miami to face England for third place after losing to Spain 2-0 in the semifinals.
While in Boston, the team practiced at Bentley University in Waltham and had rented the entire Four Seasons Hotel.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced his production company is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges in the 2022 death of her boyfriend, Officer John O’Keefe.
Multiple adaptations of Read's case are in development, including projects by Lifetime, Prime Video, and Netflix.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve and Nancy Carell celebrated her 60th birthday at Tresca, a North End Italian restaurant co-owned by Ray Bourque.
Nancy Carell, a Boston native and former 'SNL' cast member, dines at Tresca with her husband Steve Carell.
Steve and Nancy met at an improv class in Chicago and have been married since 1995, sharing screen time together.

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents reported unusually high water bills, with one nearly reaching $200,000, sparking concern among homeowners.
The issue arose during a water meter replacement, and officials are currently investigating the source of billing discrepancies.
Homeowners face penalties for unpaid bills; town officials promise legal preparations to aid those impacted by billing errors.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at Boston's Leader Bank Pavilion on July 16, featuring a 16-song setlist focused on his recent works.
Dylan's performance was described as mesmerizing, with clear vocals and unique arrangements of both his songs and select covers.
Openers Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan contributed significantly, with Spencer performing a well-received Dylan cover.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces tables in middle seats on new Airbus A321XLRs, available in Economy Plus.
The middle-seat tables feature soft leather-like covering and space for cups, enhancing comfort and storage.
Domestic flights on the A321XLR will start this fall, with international service beginning by early 2027.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The original 'Gilmore Girls' house from the pilot episode is located at 31 Victoria Ave., Markham, Ontario, Canada.
The house, listed for $2.76 million USD, features a Gothic Revival design with four bedrooms and four bathrooms.
Originally built around 1875, the property includes a 550-square-foot barn, previously used as a village bakery.
